question: [File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: responseCacheKeyLabel] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with specially ordered or encoded query parameters that collide with the cache key of another user's transaction query, causing the attacker's query to poison the cache and serve incorrect transaction data to legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: userLimitLabel] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `userLimitLabel` context value by crafting requests that overflow or underflow the per-user limit tracking, causing the rate limiter to allow more transaction queries than permitted and enabling bulk transaction enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: requestIdLabel] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted `requestId` value into the `requestIdLabel` context to cause log correlation confusion, making it appear that their malicious transaction queries are associated with legitimate request IDs in audit logs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: requestPathLabel] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request path that manipulates the `requestPathLabel` value to bypass path-based access controls or logging filters, causing transaction queries to be executed without proper audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction fee value that, when converted using `WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS` (10_000_000_000), overflows a 64-bit integer boundary, causing the fee comparison or validation to wrap around and accept a transaction with an effectively zero or negative fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS] Can an unprivileged external user submit a weibar-denominated value that, after division by `WEIBARS_TO_TINYBARS`, truncates to zero tinybars due to integer division, causing a submitted transaction to appear to have zero fees in the mirror node's records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE] Can an unprivileged external user submit an auto-renew period value that, when multiplied by `AUTO_RENEW_PERIOD_MULTIPLE` (1e9 as BigInt), overflows `MAX_LONG` (2^63-1), causing the stored auto-renew period to wrap to a negative value and trigger premature entity expiration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: MAX_LONG] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp or ID value equal to `MAX_LONG` (2^63-1) to probe whether BigInt boundary handling is consistent across all query paths, potentially causing SQL parameter binding errors that leak database schema information?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: MAX_LONG, filterKeys.TIMESTAMP] Can an unprivileged external user submit `timestamp=gte:9223372036854775807` (MAX_LONG in nanoseconds) to query transactions at the maximum possible timestamp, probing whether the system correctly handles BigInt boundary values or throws an unhandled exception that reveals internal state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: NANOSECONDS_PER_MILLISECOND] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp in milliseconds that, when multiplied by `NANOSECONDS_PER_MILLISECOND` (10^6), produces a value that overflows the expected nanosecond range, causing the timestamp filter to match transactions outside the intended time window?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: NANOS_PER_SECOND] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp with a fractional seconds component that, when converted using `NANOS_PER_SECOND`, introduces a rounding error causing the query to miss or duplicate transactions submitted at second boundaries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: EVM_ADDRESS_LENGTH] Can an unprivileged external user submit an EVM address that is shorter or longer than `EVM_ADDRESS_LENGTH` (20 bytes) with padding or truncation to exploit lenient address validation, causing the address to resolve to a different account and returning that account's submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: ETH_HASH_LENGTH] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction hash that is shorter than `ETH_HASH_LENGTH` (32 bytes) with zero-padding to probe whether the hash validation accepts truncated hashes, potentially matching multiple transactions with the same prefix?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: filterKeys.TOPIC0, filterKeys.TOPIC1, filterKeys.TOPIC2, filterKeys.TOPIC3] Can an unprivileged external user submit crafted `topic0`/`topic1`/`topic2`/`topic3` filter values to enumerate all contract log events associated with submitted transactions, reconstructing the full execution trace of smart contract transactions without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: filterKeys.TOPIC0] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `topic0` value with leading zeros stripped or added to exploit the zero-padding normalization in the topic filter (`topic.replace(/^(0x)?0*/, '')`), causing the filter to match unintended contract events from submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: filterKeys.TOPIC_ID] Can an unprivileged external user query by `topic.id` to retrieve all messages submitted to a specific topic, leaking the full submission history of consensus messages without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/constants.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Symbol: filterKeys.SERIAL_NUMBER] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate NFT serial numbers via the `serialnumber` filter to reconstruct the complete transfer history of specific NFTs, leaking which transactions submitted NFT transfers and to whom?
